comments.author: maxine-at-forecast--noether--fuzz-ci
comments.content: Plan: Implementing T1-T5 from ADR adversarial review. Order: T4 (permissions) → T5 (Cargo.lock --locked) → T2 (remove continue-on-error) → T3 (increase fuzz durations + nightly job) → T1 (new fuzz targets for comments, labels, updates, milestones, subissues, relations). docs.yml already has permissions block. Will add permissions: contents: read to ci.yml, ci-feature.yml. publish.yml needs contents: read for dry-run and publish jobs. release-builds.yml needs contents: read.

comments.author: maxine-at-forecast--noether--fuzz-ci
comments.content: Decision: For release-builds.yml, upload-artifact@v4 works with contents: read — artifact upload uses the implicit ACTIONS_RUNTIME_TOKEN, not the GITHUB_TOKEN permissions. For publish.yml, contents: read is sufficient since the crates.io publish uses CARGO_REGISTRY_TOKEN (a secret), not GitHub permissions.

comments.author: maxine-at-forecast--noether--fuzz-ci
comments.content: Decision: Added --locked to cargo build in ci.yml, ci-feature.yml, release-builds.yml, and cargo publish --dry-run in publish.yml. This ensures Cargo.lock is respected and CI fails if dependencies drift. Not adding --locked to cargo test since the build step already validates the lock file.

comments.author: maxine-at-forecast--noether--fuzz-ci
comments.content: Decision: T2+T3 combined — removed continue-on-error from all fuzz steps so crashes fail CI. Increased regular CI fuzz from 30s to 60s per target, and now running all 6 existing targets (was only 3). Created fuzz-nightly.yml with schedule cron (3 AM UTC daily) running all targets including new ones for 300s each via matrix strategy. Nightly uses fail-fast: false so one crashing target doesn't prevent others from running.

comments.author: maxine-at-forecast--noether--fuzz-ci
comments.content: Result: All T1-T5 items implemented. T4: Added permissions: contents: read to ci.yml, ci-feature.yml, publish.yml, release-builds.yml (docs.yml already had it). T5: Added --locked to cargo build in ci.yml, ci-feature.yml, release-builds.yml, and cargo publish --dry-run in publish.yml. T2: Removed continue-on-error: true from all fuzz steps — crashes now fail CI. T3: Increased fuzz duration from 30s to 60s in regular CI, created fuzz-nightly.yml with 300s/target on daily cron schedule. T1: Added 6 new fuzz targets (fuzz_comments, fuzz_labels, fuzz_update_operations, fuzz_milestones, fuzz_subissues, fuzz_relations) — total now 12 targets. Also fixed 3 compilation errors in existing targets (fuzz_cli_output add_comment arity, fuzz_state_machine start_session/get_current_session API changes). All 889 unit tests pass, clippy clean, fmt clean, all 12 fuzz targets build and run without crashes.

comments.author: maxine-at-forecast--noether--fuzz-ci
comments.content: Completed T1-T5 from ADR adversarial review. Commit 3f46757 on feature/ci-cd-fuzz-testing-improvements. 15 files changed: 4 workflow files hardened with permissions+locked, continue-on-error removed from fuzz jobs, durations increased to 60s, new fuzz-nightly.yml with 300s/target daily cron, 6 new fuzz targets added (comments, labels, update_operations, milestones, subissues, relations), 2 existing fuzz targets fixed. All 889 unit tests pass, clippy clean, fmt clean, all 12 fuzz targets build and run. Ready for push + PR to develop.

comments.author: maxine-at-forecast--noether--fuzz-ci
comments.content: Final: wrote .kickoff-status DONE marker. All work complete.
